1.14: But I didn't want to do anything without asking you first, so that whatever good you do for me will be your own free choice and not something I pressured you into.
1.6: I pray that as you share your faith with others it will become fruitful as you come to understand every good thing that belongs to us in Christ.
1.5: I keep hearing how much you love God's people and how strong your faith in the Lord Jesus is.
1.7: Your love has brought me great joy and encouragement, because the hearts of God's people have been refreshed through you, my brother.
1.9: but instead I choose to appeal to you simply on the basis of love. I, Paul, an old man now and a prisoner for Christ Jesus,
1.16: no longer as a slave, but as something far better—a beloved brother. He is very dear to me, but even more so to you—both as a man and as a fellow believer.
1.1: I, Paul, am writing this letter from prison for Christ Jesus. I'm joined by our brother Timothy. I'm writing to you, Philemon, our dear friend and partner in ministry—
1.2: and also to our sister Apphia, to Archippus who fights alongside us, and to the whole church that gathers in your home.
1.20: So, my brother, let me have this benefit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
1.18: If he has hurt you in any way or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
1.15: Perhaps he was taken from you for a short time for this very reason: so you could have him back permanently—
1.22: One more thing: please prepare a guest room for me, because I hope to be given back to you through your prayers.
1.25: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1.12: I'm sending him back to you, and in doing so I'm sending you my own heart.
1.8: Because of our union with Christ I could order you to do what I'm about to ask—
1.3: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
1.19: I, Paul, am writing this personally: I will pay you back. (I won't mention, of course, that you owe me your very life!)
1.24: So do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke—my fellow workers.
1.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner for Christ Jesus, sends his greetings.
1.17: If you consider me your partner, then welcome him as you would welcome me.
1.11: In the past, as his name suggests, he was useless to you—but now he has become truly useful, both to you and to me.
1.13: I would have loved to keep him here to help me while I'm in chains for the gospel—he could have served in your place.
1.10: am appealing to you on behalf of Onesimus—my own son in the faith, born while I've been here in prison.
1.21: I'm writing with full confidence that you will do what I ask—in fact, I think you'll do even more.
1.4: Every time I pray for you, I thank God for you.
